properties.remark: *** 1 FATAL *** TWO INDIVIDUALS WERE CANYONEERING IN WATER SLIDE CANYON NEAR CHERRY CREEK. PEAK FLOWS ON CHERRY CREEK WERE ABOUT 3-6 FEET HIGHER THAN BASE FLOW. ONE INDIVIDUAL DROWNED. HIS REMAINS WERE FOUND ON FEB. 24 AT 11:30AM.
VERDE LAKES DR. CLOSED DUE TO FLOODING OF WEST CLEAR CREEK. REPORT VIA NWS FLAGSTAFF FACEBOOK. PHOTOS CONFIRM FLOODING STILL CONTINUES AT AROUND 6 PM.
PUBLIC REPORTS 4 FOOT RISE ON EAST VERDE RIVER AT EAST VERDE ESTATES.
CORRECTS PREVIOUS NON-TSTM WND GST REPORT TO TSTM WND GST. ASOS STATION KINW WINSLOW AIRPORT.
A HANGER AT THE VALLE AIRPORT WAS LIFTED AND MOVED 100 YARDS EARLY SATURDAY MORNING. THIS WAS LIKELY DUE TO A DOWNBURST.
ADOT WEATHER SENSOR ON I-40 @ PAINTED DESERT NP HQ (AZT13) MEASURED A 64 MPH WIND GUST.

CORRECTS PREVIOUS TORNADO REPORT FROM 5 NNW VALLE TO CHANGE THE DATE OF OCCURRENCE FROM 7/24 TO 7/23. LANDSPOUT TORNADO REPORTED BY THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND CONFIRMED WITH VIDEO. LOCATION IS ROUGH ESTIMATE.
LANDSPOUT TORNADO REPORTED BY THE GENERAL PUBLIC AND CONFIRMED WITH VIDEO. LOCATION IS ROUGH ESTIMATE.
USGS STREAM GAUGE CRZA3 ON CARRIZO CREEK REPORTED A 2.4 FT RISE IN WATER.
FLOODING REPORTED ALONG IR 15 BETWEEN SUNRISE SPRINGS AND BURNSIDE AROUND MILE POST 94 TO 95. ESTIMATED 5-10 INCHES OF WATER AND MUD COVERING THE ROAD. TIME AND LOCATION ARE BEST ESTIMATES.
ON SILVER ROAD.
TREE LIMBS DOWN. SEVERAL WEATHER STATIONS IN THE AREA RECORDED GUSTS 60-68 MPH.
SPOTTER Y 116 REPORTED A DUST STORM NORTH OF PAULDEN, VISIBILITY 1.5 MILES AND NNW WIND SUSTAINED AT 29 MPH FROM DAVIS WX STATION.
MESONET STATION QSPA3 SUNSET POINT.
NICKEL SIZED HAIL, STRONG WINDS AND HEAVY RAIN.
FROM FACEBOOK, A PHOTO OF ESTIMATED 1 INCH HAIL STONES IN THE PERSONS HAND.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS ONE INCH HAIL.
WINDOW BROKEN BY FALLEN POLE. POWER OUT IN TOWN. TWO 30-FT TRAVEL TRAILERS FLIPPED. TIME ESTIMATED.
THE GRANITE CREEK GAUGE (GCRA3) ROSE 2.9 FEET IN AN HOUR DUE TO HEAVIER RAINFALL FROM AN EARLIER THUNDERSTORM.
A MEMBER OF THE PUBLIC AND A COUNTY WORKER BOTH ESTIMATED A 3-4 FOOT RISE OF MUDDY BLACK WATER ON SLATE CREEK. TIME IS ESTIMATED BASED ON RADAR PRECIPITATION.
TRAINED SPOTTER REPORTS 1 INCH HAIL.
A GAUGE AT THE AUGA FRIA AT OLD BLACK CANYON HIGHWAY ROSE 1.8 FT.
